steps.prompt: Which word is the main verb?

hints.to lead: That would be 'ducere' — 'Dedit' comes from 'dare' (to give)
hints.to say: That would be 'dicere' — 'Dedit' comes from 'dare' (to give)
hints.to put: That would be 'ponere' — 'Dedit' comes from 'dare' (to give)

hints.present indicative: Present of 'dare' would be 'dat' — 'Dedit' with the reduplicated stem is perfect
hints.perfect subjunctive: Perfect subjunctive would be 'dederit' — 'Dedit' is perfect indicative

hints.duco, ducere, duxi, ductum: These are the parts of 'ducere' (to lead)
hints.dico, dicere, dixi, dictum: These are the parts of 'dicere' (to say)
hints.doceo, docere, docui, doctum: These are the parts of 'docere' (to teach)

hints.nominative: Nominative plural would be 'illi' — 'illis' is dative/ablative plural
hints.ablative: While 'illis' could be ablative, with 'dare' (to give to) it is dative (indirect object)
hints.accusative: Accusative plural would be 'illos' — 'illis' is dative here

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'virtus' — 'virtutem' with -em is accusative
hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'virtute' — 'virtutem' is accusative, direct object

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'virtutis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'virtutis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'virtutis' ends in -is

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'potestas' — 'potestatem' with -em is accusative

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'potestatis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'potestatis' ends in -is
hints.5th declension: 5th declension has genitive in -ei — 'potestatis' ends in -is

hints.sed (but): 'Sed' means 'but' — 'et' here connects two coordinate accusative objects
hints.aut (or): 'Aut' means 'or' — 'et' connects the two nouns as a pair

translation: He gave them power and authority
